description:
## Goal
Neo and hawk currently run as ephemeral K8s Jobs spun up by ordis per task. This means they can't receive asynchronous BeadHub messages or participate in ongoing conversations from #agent-comms. Make them persistent Deployments like the orchestrator.

## Design
- **neo** (developer): Persistent Deployment in beadhub namespace, polls BeadHub for pending messages, invokes claude with task context
- **hawk** (reviewer): Same pattern, reviewer role
- Both register in the control-plane project on startup via `bdh :init`
- Both have access to `bd` for ticket tracking after init
- Working directory: /home/node/work — reset per task (clone repo specified in task message)
- Message routing: BeadHub sessions → appear in #agent-comms threads automatically (existing bridge handles this)

## Acceptance Criteria
- neo and hawk pods are always running in beadhub namespace
- Woodson can send a message to neo or hawk directly from #agent-comms and get a reply
- Ordis can message neo or hawk via bdh and get a response without spinning up a Job
- Messages between agents appear in appropriate #agent-comms threads
title: Create persistent neo and hawk worker pods

description:
## Problem
The local Dolt database for bd issue tracking was corrupted on the orchestrator pod. Running `bd list` produced: 'database hq_beadhub not found on Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:3307'. Only config.yaml existed in .beads/dolt/.

## Root Cause
Likely caused by an unclean shutdown or pod restart during Dolt server operation, leaving the .beads/dolt/ directory in an inconsistent state.

## Fix Applied
Ran `rm -rf .beads/dolt && bd init --force --prefix hq-beadhub` to reinitialize the Dolt database. This is a fresh database — existing tickets were lost (none were recorded prior to this session).

## Prevention
Consider persisting the Dolt database via a PersistentVolumeClaim on the orchestrator pod, or using a remote Dolt server instead of the embedded local server.
title: bd Dolt database corruption on orchestrator pod
